name: FORCE1s
description: Positive-going variant evolved from JEDI-2P with substantially larger spike responses, high intrinsic brightness, and repolarization kinetics tuned for 2P imaging.

spectrum.name: FORCE1s
spectrum.note: 1P excitation/emission curve is inherited byte-identical from the parent JEDI-2P, which itself carries the cpGFP curve shared across the ASAP/JEDI lineage (digitized from the ASAP4e paper, doi:10.1038/s41592-023-01913-z, Ext. Fig. 3e). FORCE1s' VSD/chromophore mutations leave the 1P lineshape essentially unchanged: the FORCE1s preprint shows the FORCE1s emission spectrum in Fig. S1.2A and states the FORCE1s, JEDI-2P, and ASAP4e excitation/emission spectra are 'qualitatively similar'. Fig. S1.2A reports a measured FORCE1s 1P emission peak of 516 ± 5 nm (vs 519 ± 3 nm JEDI-2P, 518 ± 4 nm ASAP4e, 511 ± 1 nm EGFP; HEK293A cells excited at 430/10 nm), so peakEm is set to 516. No 1P excitation peak is numerically declared for FORCE1s, so peakEx (489 nm) and the custom curve lineshape are taken from the parent JEDI-2P. FORCE1s does not yet have a dedicated FPbase entry.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean of the multi-exponential fits at 33°C under widefield 1P illumination (HEK293A whole-cell voltage clamp, n=14; Table S1.4). Activation (−70→+30 mV): τfast 2.8 ms (93%) + τslow 16 ms (7%) → τ̄ = 0.93×2.8 + 0.07×16 = 3.72 ms. Deactivation (+30→−70 mV): τfast 2.4 ms (47%) + τslow 9.1 ms (53%) → τ̄ = 0.47×2.4 + 0.53×9.1 = 5.95 ms. (The traces were actually best fit by a triple exponential whose third term is a slow photobleaching component excluded from Table S1.4, so the two reported kinetic components define the weighting.) Previously stored the fast components alone (2.8 / 2.4); corrected to the weighted means per policy. For context, in vivo FORCE1s-Kv repolarization was 2.9 ± 0.7 ms (Fig. 3D), ~3× slower than JEDI-2P-Kv.

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F0 for a true 100-mV step (-70 → +30 mV), read off the steady-state voltage-response curve (Fig. 1E; FORCE1s green point at +30 mV ≈ 267%, re-digitized 2026-07-03 and cross-checked against the paper's stated anchors of 87.4% at -70→-40 mV and 227% at -70→0 mV, both of which the digitized curve reproduces within ±1%). Whole-cell voltage clamp with 2P imaging at 33°C in HEK293A cells (n = 7). The paper's headline number, 227 ± 34%, is the -70 → 0 mV (70-mV) step, not a 100-mV step; the -70 → -40 mV (30-mV) step was 87.4 ± 13.9% (Fig. 1F, Fig. S1.2C).

sensitivityData.note: Mean single-spike (isolated AP) response amplitude of FORCE1s-Kv in Layer 2/3 V1 neurons of awake, head-fixed mice under ULoVE 2P microscopy (92.7 ± 24.9% ΔF/F, mean ± SD; 3.4-fold larger than JEDI-2P-Kv's 27.6 ± 4.1%).

photostabilityData.note: In vitro (HEK293A). 100 mW power at the sample plane under 2P resonant scanning at 940 nm over a 40 × 635 µm FOV for 6 min (Fig. S1.2B). Read off the FORCE1s (green) normalized-2P-fluorescence curve at the 360-s (6-min) endpoint ≈ 0.57, just below ASAP4e (≈0.60) and well above JEDI-2P (≈0.32). The paper reports a sample-plane POWER, not an irradiance, so it is stored as a power (mW) and will not normalize to the mW/mm² standard. Previously stored 87% — inconsistent with the figure (the curve is already ~0.80 by ~30 s and ~0.57 by 6 min); corrected.

In vivo (awake mouse V1 L2/3) under FACED2.0 2P microscopy at 256 Hz. FORCE1s-Kv retained 86 ± 6% of initial fluorescence across 22 trials spanning 180 s (3 min) (mean ± SD, n = 154 neurons; Fig. 4B/4E). Power/irradiance not reported as an mW/mm² value, so this entry is display-only and does not normalize to the standard.

In vivo, freely moving mice (V1 L2/3) using a MINI2P miniature 2P microscope. FORCE1s-Kv retained 72 ± 9% of baseline fluorescence at the end of 40-min sessions (mean ± SEM, n = 5 neurons). Laser power 50-65 mW measured out of the objective (a power, not an irradiance), so this entry is display-only and does not normalize.

photobleach.note: Model-free photostability: t75% = time for fluorescence to fall to 75% of initial. Digitized from the FORCE1s curve in Fig. S1.2B (2P resonance-scanning, 940 nm, 100 mW at the sample plane over a 40x635 um FOV, 6 min, HEK293A; normalized 2P fluorescence). 2P power-only measurement (not mW/mm2) so it stands alone and is NOT intensity-normalized or comparable to 1P GEVIs. Measured alongside FORCE1s/ASAP4e/JEDI-2P in the same panel (they cross-overlay).

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, visual cortex L2/3, awake head-fixed, in vivo (ULoVE 2P), soma-targeted FORCE1s-Kv
apWidthData.note: Optical spike full width at half maximum of FORCE1s-Kv single spikes recorded in vivo under ULoVE 2P microscopy: 2.6 ± 0.5 ms (mean ± SD; n = 9 cells), 1.9-fold larger than JEDI-2P-Kv (1.4 ± 0.3 ms, n = 17) measured in the same panel (Fig. 3E). Recorded in layer-2/3 V1 of awake head-fixed mice at 7.1 kHz ULoVE acquisition (10-min recordings); body temperature ~37°C. FWHM = time extent at half-maximum amplitude of the isolated-spike spike-triggered average waveform, after 20 kHz linear interpolation (Methods). Consistent with the in-vivo repolarization τoff of 2.9 ± 0.7 ms (Fig. 3D). Distinct from the 2-ms FWHM electrical command waveform used in vitro (Fig. 1G), which is the injected input shape, not the optical response.

apWidthData.sample: HEK293A cells, whole-cell voltage clamp, in vitro (2-ms FWHM command AP waveform)
apWidthData.note: In-vitro optical response width: FWHM of FORCE1s' fluorescence response to an isolated 2-ms-FWHM, 100-mV command spike waveform under whole-cell voltage clamp + 2P imaging at 33°C, HEK293A (n = 11). Imaging by resonant-scanning at 721 Hz. Digitized from the Fig. 1K dot plot (y-axis 2-6 ms, 95% CI bars): FORCE1s mean ≈ 4.9 ms (±~0.1 ms), wider than ASAP4e (≈4.2 ms) and JEDI-2P (≈3.6 ms) measured in the same panel, consistent with FORCE1s' slower off-kinetics. NOTE: this width is the optical response to a synthetic 2-ms command input (not a real spike), so it is broadened by both the command width and the sensor kinetics — distinct from the in-vivo real-spike FWHM of 2.6 ms (Fig. 3E, 7.1 kHz). No exact number is printed; value is digitized.

twoPhoton.note: FORCE1s was engineered and validated specifically for 2P voltage imaging. The 2P excitation spectrum (Fig. 1B) shows a broad profile with >90% of peak excitation between 930-1000 nm (comparable peaks at 940 and 990 nm; 26% of peak at 1030 nm). All in vitro characterization (Fig. 1) and in vivo recordings (resonant-scanning, ULoVE, diagonal scanning, FACED2.0, and MINI2P freely-moving) were performed under 2P excitation, typically at 940 nm.

researchPapers.title: A versatile, positive-going voltage indicator that enables accessible two-photon recordings in vivo
researchPapers.authors: McDonald AJ, Land MA, Yang S, et al.
researchPapers.sample: Mouse, visual cortex L2/3, in vivo; Mouse, visual cortex, freely moving, in vivo; HEK293 cells, in vitro; Rat, hippocampal neurons, in vitro
